STORYLINE

Mary had always been a problematic child, unafraid to voice her opinion and get physical if needed. She grew up in the slums of London, with her family trying their best to give her a better life with school education. She wasn't ungrateful, but she was also too stubborn and unwilling to conform to the school disciplinary system, often times bitching about anything she didn't like.

This habit only stuck with Mary all the way up to her young adult years. Now with a choice on how to live and what to do she saw America, the place of dreams supposedly. Mary quickly went blindly into it, securing the required needs to move. She started off with getting a job, nothing outstanding but it was a start. She went through her middle twenties trying online dating, something relatively new at the time, and she was keen to find her match. It wouldn't work so well for Mary though, sure she had the looks, but her bitchy personality and outlook on life wasn't doing her any favors, constantly being a turn away for any potential lovers.

Her bitchiness only worsened with each failed relationship, each one ending with the same tendency: Lack of physical attraction. That's right, all of Mary's relationship would soon end after the physical spark was gone, every lover always losing their tolerance for her, and shortly after either cheating or ending the relationship all together.

Because of this her bitchiness was now not only who she was but also became a shield for her battered heart, pushing away any potential harm, and locking away the real person inside. She felt alone in a world that couldn't love her, couldn't accept her, and she soon learned to adapt to it. Now present day and being single for 6 years, Mary's not certain she's better off, both mentally and physically, but at her age she wasn't sure if such trivialities in her eyes matter anymore.
